17.gl: exiled; captured; as noun, exile (abstractly or concretely and collectively); by extension, booty
17.kj: captive(-ity), prisoners, [idiom] take away, that was taken.

17.gl: Jehudah (or Judah), the name of five Israelites; also of the tribe descended from the first, and of its territory
an Edomite, or descendants from (or inhabitants of) Edom
17.dv: or (fully) אֱדוֹמִי ; patronymic from H123 (אֱדֹם); See H726 (אֲרוֹמִי).

18.gl: properly, to sit down (specifically as judge. in ambush, in quiet); by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry
18.kj: (make to) abide(-ing), continue, (cause to, make to) dwell(-ing), ease self, endure, establish, [idiom] fail, habitation, haunt, (make to) inhabit(-ant), make to keep (house), lurk

18.gl: to spread out (i.e. deploy in hostile array); by analogy, to strip (i.e. unclothe, plunder, flay, etc.)
18.kj: fall upon, flay, invade, make an invasion, pull off, put off, make a road, run upon, rush, set, spoil, spread selves (abroad), strip (off, self).

18.gl: a daughter (used in the same wide sense as other terms of relationship, literally and figuratively)
18.kj: apple (of the eye), branch, company, daughter, [idiom] first, [idiom] old, [phrase] owl, town, village.

18.gl: there (transferring to time) then; often thither, or thence
18.kj: in it, [phrase] thence, there (-in, [phrase] of, [phrase] out), [phrase] thither, [phrase] whither.
18.dv: a primitive particle (rather from the relative pronoun, H834 (אֲשֶׁר));

18.gl: a city (a place guarded by waking or a watch) in the widest sense (even of a mere encampment or post)
18.kj: Ai (from margin), city, court (from margin), town.
18.dv: or (in the plural) עָר; or עָיַר; (Judges 10:4), from H5782 (עוּר)

18.kj: [idiom] at all, catch (self), be frozen, be holden, stick together, take.

19.dv: or עָבֻר; passive participle of H5674 (עָבַר);

Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
19.kj: Jehovah, the Lord. Compare H3050 (יָהּ), H3069 (יְהֹוִה).
